zh.lifeSummary: Ellen de Rossel於1570年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾克倫威爾。她於1587年5月13日與John Rossell在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾可洛普維爾 畢曉普結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1625年在她的家鄉享年55歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ellen de Rossel于1570年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾克倫威爾。她于1587年5月13日与John Rossell在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾可洛普維爾 畢曉普结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1625年在她的家乡享年55岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rossel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Fernando, Juan, Alvaro, Ana, Carols, Catarino, Guillermo, Javier, Jose, Orlando, Pedro, Ruben.  French Philippe, Yves.  Dutch Gerrit, Flor.
French, Dutch, and Flemish: nickname for a red-haired man, from a diminutive of Old French and Dutch ros ‘red’.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Catalan   Rossell  .

variants.name: Roessel
meanings: German (Rössel): nickname, or perhaps an occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle High German rossel, a diminutive of ros ‘charger, draft horse’; otherwise a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with this word, for example Zum weissen Rössl (‘At the Little White Horse’). Compare Rossel .
German (Rössel): habitational name from Rössel in East Prussia. Compare Rossel .
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic; Rössel): from a diminutive of German Ross ‘horse’, one of surnames assigned at random by Austrian clerks.

variants.name: Roszel
meanings: German: archaic variant of   Rossel  . This form of the surname is no longer found in Germany. Compare   Rossell   and   Roszell  .

variants.name: Rozzelle
meanings: Altered form of   Rossel   or   Rossell  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Rozzell  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rosselot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre.
French: diminutive of   Rossel  .

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Ellen de Rossel was born in 1570, in Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. She married John Rossell on 13 May 1587, in Cropwell Bishop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1625, in her hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Ellen de Rossel est née en 1570, à Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé John Rossell le 13 mai 1587, à Cropwell Bishop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1625, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Ellen de Rosselは1570年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクロムウエルで生まれた。1587年5月13日にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクロプウエル ビショップでJohn Rossellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1625年に故郷で55歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ellen de Rossel(은)는 1570년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 크롬웰에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1587년 5월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 크럽웰 비숍에서 John Rossell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1625년 그녀의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Ellen de Rossel родилась в 1570 г. в местности кромвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Rossell 13 мая 1587 г. в местности кропвелл бишоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1625 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.
